_doc: Registry for tools/bench_node.sh — the repeatable bench-node runner. THIS file is the public-safe TEMPLATE (placeholders only). Copy it to tools/bench_nodes.json (GITIGNORED) and fill in REAL tailnet IPs / ssh users / repo paths there. Only 'sanitized_name' may ever appear in a committed artifact. Mirrors the fleet_endpoints.example.json convention.
_safety: tools/bench_nodes.json is gitignored — real identity (tailnet_ip, ssh_user, magicdns, repo_path, host_key) lives ONLY there.
Reachability is an SSH handshake, not ICMP/tailscale ping (a node can answer ping with no sshd).
host_key is PINNED here after bootstrap — the driver verifies against it instead of blind accept-new.
Every run writes lineage.json (utc datetime + fak version/commit + node + go) for traceability.

Results go to tools/_registry/bench-runs/ (gitignored). Promoting a result to a committed path needs a REAL redaction pass (CPU brand + go/git + hostname) — the shape-only scrub audit does NOT do that.

_onboarding._doc: Public-safe roster of the bench nodes this runner targets and where each stands (#922). The HARDWARE-CATALOG node roster is private; this block is the committed, sanitized view. 'pending' nodes have a placeholder entry below — onboarding = copy it into the gitignored tools/bench_nodes.json, fill in REAL identity, get the driver pubkey into the node's authorized_keys, pin host_key (ssh-keyscan -t ed25519), then `bench_node.sh <node> tests`.

roster.note: Apple M3 Pro arm64 — tests green, BenchmarkDecide ~317 ns idle.

roster.blocker: host-reachability — desktop answers ICMP but ships with no sshd; enable sshd + add driver pubkey, then pin host_key.

remote-shell-mismatch — host reachable + key-auth OK over Tailscale (RTX 4070, Ada/sm_89), but its SSH login shell is Windows cmd (a remote `uname` returns 'not recognized'), so the runner's `bash -s` model can't execute there; set WSL bash as the SSH login shell (or add a Windows-shell adapter to bench_node.sh), THEN fill the gitignored registry + verify.
ephemeral-host — cloud VM IP + host_key rotate on each provision and the driver's ADC may be stale; (re)bootstrap per provision, then pin.
no-direct-ssh — DGX is reached via a relay bridge, not a direct tailnet SSH handshake; needs a reachable sshd path before the driver can poll it.
